entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud enables user-based authoriza...
entry.content:
Hi Kerisha,

I also create a ticket to SAP in parallel.

The expert provides the following information. It looks the Sales Order Net Value field is not part of the price elements area controlled by access levels. I'll further align with developer to check if there is solution to cover the requirements/

You can find the detailed feedback as below.

Regarding your business requirement, after debugging in SAP Backend, I created the documentation below to explain to you the behavior of our coding logic regarding the view restriction of fields:

  • 0003744032 - Authorization restrictions in the field Net Value - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

As per SAP standard documentation, role-based access to price elements applies only to pricing elements in the price elements area such as condition types and assigned subtotals (for example discounts, internal cost, margin).

Sales Order Net Value field (header and item) is a document total and is not part of the price elements area controlled by access levels. Therefore, Net Value cannot be hidden via access level configuration. This behavior applies consistently in Manage Sales Orders – Version 2 as well as in the Create Sales Orders - VA01 app.

If an user is authorized to display or approve a sales order, the Net Value will be visible by design. There is no standard configuration, authorization object, or authorization check in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to suppress Net Value visibility for sales orders. 

Since development tracks the customers ideas via the SAP Customer Influence portal I kindly ask you to create a feature request for this business requirement.

Best regards,

Joey

entry.title: Configuring custom GRC NWBC Launch Screens restricted via PFCG roles
entry.content:
Challenge: Standard SAP GRC roles for NetWeaver Business Client (NWBC) grant excessive access privileges that exceed users' actual job requirements. This over-provisioning creates security risks and poor user experience, as users encounter numerous irrelevant hyperlinks and folders they don't need for their daily tasks.

Implementation Approach: The solution leverages transaction LPD_CUST (Configure Launchpad for Menus) to design custom NWBC landing pages. This involves copying standard Webdynpro application configuration, component configuration and UIBB component in which LPD_CUST role is linked.

In this blog, the detailed steps for configuring LPD_CUST, Web Dynpro Configuration and PFCG mapping is highlighted.

Environment: GRC Access Control 12.0

Launchpad Flow chart:

 

Section 1: LPD_CUST launchpad customisation

In Tcode LPD_CUST, we need to define the customization that is required for the workset, here we have considered GRACHOME (GRC Access Control Home Workset). It is recommended to take a copy of this workset to custom naming convention to preserve standard configuration.

Select line item GRACHOME, Click on copy.

 

 

Enter your custom values and namespace.

 

 

In ZGRACHOME, You can make all the required customisations. Here, for simplicity, I have modified the folder name to ‘Work Inbox - CUSTOM’.

Potential Use case 1: For Approvers users, you can keep only ‘Work Inbox’, Delete ‘Work Inbox -  Simplified’ to make it more clear to users as to which one to use.

Potential Use case 2: In Application help, You can place company’s intranet URL link that points to a guide on how to review and approve requests. For this, you can create ‘New Application’ , select application type ‘URL’ and add the required URL.

 

You can follow the same steps to customize Setup, Access Management, Reports and Analytics Webdynpro Application

 

Section 2: NWBC Webdynpro customisation

In tcode PFCG, copy standard role SAP_GRAC_NWBC to custom role Z_TEST_NWBC,

Tcode PFCG -> Z_TEST_NWBC

Here, we are customizing the ‘My Home’ Webdynpro application GRFN_SERVICE_MAP.

Right click on ‘My Home’ Webdynpro application -> Details

We see the standard Configuration: GRAC_FPM_AC_LPD_HOME. We want to customize this Application Configuration to point our desired ‘My Home’ Layout in NWBC which we configured in LPD_CUST.

Click on ‘Application Configuration’ button.

 

This will launch a web URL which takes you to application configuration.

 

 

From here on, the logic is that we copy the Application Configuration, Component Configuration, UIBB Configuration and In UIBB configuration we point it to Role /instance of LPD_CUST. Refer to the flowchart.

Step 1: Create Copy of Application Configuration.

 
Click on Other Functions -> New Window -> Copy

 

 

 

Provide custom configuration ID and description

 

Select the relevant package, here I chose $TMP (Non-transportable), Click OK.

 

 

Click Change

 

We enter into the custom application configuration Z1GRAC_FPM_AC_LPD_HOME

Click on hyper link GRAC_FPM_CC_LPD_HOME

 

You will reach Component Configuration screen as below

 

Step 2: Create copy of component configuration

Click on Other Functions -> New Window -> Copy

 

Provide the custom configuration ID and description

 

Enter the desired package

 

Click Change

 

Click on Edit -> Cancel to get in Display mode, Click on hyperlink ‘GRAC_FPM_UIBB_LPD_HOME’

 

Step 3: Create copy of UIBB configuration

In this window, Click on Other Functions -> New Window

Click Copy

 

Provide the custom configuration ID and description

 

Click Change.

In this window, we need to map ZGRACHOME in Role field (earlier created in LPD_CUST).

Change it to ZGRACHOME. All the customizations done in ZGRACHOME can be previewed. Notice that we had changed folder ‘Work Inbox – CUSTOM’ in this scenario.

Click Save.

Select the TR and Click OK

 

Section 3: PFCG Role Configuration mapping

Step 1: In tcode PFCG, Role Z_TEST_NWBC

Right click on ‘My Home’ -> Details -> Change configuration to Z1GRAC_FPM_AC_LPD_HOME

Click Save

 

Step 2: Again, Right click on ‘My Home’ -> Details -> Click Application Configuration.

 

This will open up URL in web browser, we see that we are in custom application configuration Z1GRAC_FPM_AC_LPD_HOME, we see standard component configuration is mapped, we will need to change to custom component configuration Z1GRAC_FPM_CC_LPD_HOME

 

Click on Edit -> Assign Configuration Name

Assign the custom component configuration ID Z1GRAC_FPM_CC_LPD_HOME

Click Save.

 

Step 3: Click the hyperlink Z1GRAC_FPM_CC_LPD_HOME

 

This will open up Custom component configuration, we see that standard UIBB is mapped, but we need to change to custom UIBB Z1GRAC_FPM_UIBB_LPD_HOME

 

Click on F4 button on GRAC_FPM_UIBB_LPD_HOME, change it to Z1GRAC_FPM_UIBB_LPD_HOME

Click Save

 

Step 4: Click on Edit -> Cancel. We are in display mode. Click on hyperlink Z1GRAC_FPM_UIBB_LPD_HOME

 

You will find all the elements mapped as per the flowchart

 

Assign the PFCG role Z_TEST_NWBC to user and test the changes. Or you can Right click on ‘My Home’ -> Execute.

 

The customisation done in LPD_CUST reflects. 

NOTE: If you assign standard role SAP_GRAC_NWBC and custom role Z_TEST_NWBC together to a user, this won’t work as intended since the system is confused whether to pick up the standard application configuration or the custom application configuration defined in the Webdynpro application GRFN_SERVICE_MAP. The intent of creating the custom role is to assign that specific custom role Z_TEST_NWBC to satisfy all NWBC access requirements for the user.

You can follow the same steps to customize Setup, Access Management, Reports and Analytics Webdynpro Application.

 

Conclusion:

You have now successfully created a custom PFCG role with Web Dynpro application configuration pointing to Custom NWBC screen.

entry.title: Re: AM_ASSET - Archiving for a specific asset number
entry.content: No, it is not possible to enable the asset number field in Production Mode for AM_ASSET archiving. SAP intentionally restricts that field to test runs only. In productive runs, the system enforces residence time and retention rules, so you cannot bypass them by selecting specific asset numbers.

entry.title: Re: Workflow Trigger for Credit Memo (Document Type DG) i...
entry.content:
Good day @rohini_dutta21 

Thank you for your question.

Question 1: Could you please confirm whether this requirement is feasible within the standard Public Cloud framework?

Answer: No, your requirement is not feasible within the standard Public Cloud framework. There is no supported standard workflow for documents posted through Create Outgoing Invoices using the Credit Memo option with document DG document typ.  However, output configuration for FI customer invoices which is posted from Create Outgoing Invoices and Upload Customer Open Items is supported in Public Cloud. So, if you are looking to send emails, forms, or output after posting, that is the standard area you should check. DG is the accounting document type for Customer Credit Memo in the relevant accounting document type mappings.

See the following references for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/dfd480bf588447e3afa2bca80b5d21ad.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/73fe476d42454f5a95b1ee0ae7498d5e.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/26b51d1773a04bcea7d2cc029bca45b4/63576ca65b74494e9170db9cc023eea1.html?locale=en-US

 

Question 2: If not, is there any planned roadmap item or enhancement that would support this functionality in future releases?

Answer: There is currently no publicized roadmap that specifically adds a standard workflow for Credit Memo/DG documents. I would not assume this functionality is planned.

Summary/recommendation:

  • If your business requirement is notification or document dispatch after your posting, then evaluate standard output management as the supported alternative.

  • If you require a true approval or post creation workflow, you can raise it through Customer Influence.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: SAP Ariba Category Management 2605 Release Highlights
entry.content:
Hello SAP Ariba Source to Contract Community, and welcome to this latest blog post on the exciting updates coming to SAP Ariba Category Management. We're excited to announce the latest updates to SAP Ariba Category Management in Q2 2026. These enhancements are designed with one goal in mind: to give you more control, visibility, and flexibility in how you manage your source-to-contract processes.

Let's walk through what's new and how these features can help your organization work smarter.

1. Versioning of Strategy and Plan Documents for Updates to Custom Tools [ARBCATA-570]

When you update custom tools in your strategy and plan documents, you need to know exactly what changed and when. Our new versioning feature for strategy and plan documents does exactly that.

Now, every time a category manager updates a custom tool that's part of an approved document, a new version is automatically created and tracked. Category management administrators can even decide whether these changes should trigger a reapproval process - giving you the governance you need without unnecessary bottlenecks.

The result is complete visibility into document history and consistent version control across all your strategy and planning tools.

 

2. Enhancements to Risk Management in the Risk Assessment Tool [ARBCATA-573]

Managing risk doesn't happen in isolation. That's why we've enhanced the Risk Assessment tool with smarter collaboration features.

Risk owners can now create and assign tasks directly within the tool, making it easier to track mitigation activities and hold teams accountable. We've also introduced new permissions that let collaborators manage risks and tasks assigned to them—with email and in-app notifications triggered whenever ownership changes.

This means faster follow-ups, clearer accountability, and better visibility into your risk mitigation efforts.

A new permission, Can manage own risks, is now available when adding collaborators to strategy and plan documents. You can assign this permission to users who are responsible for handling risks.

 

A new section, Tasks, is now available in the risk page of the Risk Assessment tool. As a risk owner or a user with the Can manage own risks permission, you can create new tasks and assign them to other users.

 

3. Ability for Category Managers to Manage Category Profile Data [ARBCATA-583]

Category management shouldn't bottleneck at one person. With our new category profile management capability, any category manager on your team can now create, edit, and manage category profile data—not just the lead manager. Simply add team members and assign the right permissions, and they'll have the access they need to keep your category information current and accurate.

 

 

4. Make Category Profile Data Work Across Your Organization

Your category profile contains valuable information - regulations, policies, spend channels and it should not be siloed. We've added two powerful capabilities:

a. Inclusion of Category Profile Data in Strategy and Plan Documents [ARBCATA-589]

Category profile data now flows directly into your strategy and plan documents, eliminating manual work and reducing errors.

 

b. Apply Category Profile Information Across Subcategories [ARBCATA-610]

You can now associate and inherit profile information—like laws, regulations, and policies—from parent categories to subcategories automatically, saving time and ensuring consistency across your entire category hierarchy.

A new action button, Apply to Subcategories, is now available to apply the respective category profile information to subcategories.

 

5. Ability to Associate Multiple Guided Sourcing Projects with Initiatives and Initiative Tasks [ARBCATA-591]

If you're using SAP Ariba Sourcing with guided sourcing capability, you now have more flexibility in how you organize your work.

You can now associate multiple guided sourcing projects with initiatives and tasks, giving you a cleaner way to organize and track sourcing activities without creating separate initiatives for every project. This keeps your initiative management simpler and your sourcing activities better aligned.

 

6. Support for Integration with External Spend Data APIs [ARBCATA-642]

Many organizations maintain their own spend data systems, and copying that data into SAP Ariba Category Management is tedious and error-prone.

With external spend data API integration, you can now connect your own spend data source directly to SAP Ariba Category Management. Once configured, the system automatically retrieves and displays your spend information—at category, subcategory, supplier, and classification levels—without any manual intervention.

This means faster insights, less duplicate effort, and better spend visibility using data you already trust.

The UI Card Mapping dropdown label under the Integrations tab is now updated to Spend Card Mapping.

 

A new section, Spend Data, is now available in the Integrations >Spend Card Mapping page. The Spend Data section includes a spend data source selection option, with available choices of Procurement data warehouse and a Customer-defined spend data source.

 

A new section, Spend Data, is now available in the Integrations >Spend Card Mapping page. The Spend Data section includes a spend data source selection option, with available choices of Procurement data warehouse and a Customer-defined spend data source.

These updates reflect our commitment to building solutions that adapt to how you work - but not the other way around. So go ahead and review these items.

And, stay tuned for more enhancements and continue to leverage SAP Ariba Category Management to drive business success!

entry.title: 🇮🇳 Joule Studio: Create Joule Skills and Agents Without Coding (Hyderabad, India)
entry.content:
This is the all-new SAP CodeJam for Joule Studio, a new tool in SAP Build that lets you create extensions to Joule so users can use SAP's digital assistant in their custom workflows.

The CodeJam is intended to introduce you to the Joule Studio tool so you can create your own:

  • Skills, which let you create predefined execution flows, triggered by a user's prompt inside the Joule chatbot
  • Agents, which lets you define complex scenarios that the agent can execute by iteratively reasoning and interacting with their environment to achieve a goal, using a selected LLM and triggered by a user's prompt inside the Joule chatbot

The session will include an introduction to the SAP AI tools and some basic concepts needed for working with Joule Studio—such as Generative AI, AI Core, AI Launchpad, document grounding, embeddings, and what is an LLM? etc. 

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser of either Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge. Other browsers are not supported.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have, to have the instructions on a separate screen.

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Friday, June 26, 2026, from 10:00 - 16:00 local time in Hyderabad, India (IST). The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Event Address: Tata Consultancy Services, Blocks SDB1 & SDB2, Sy. No. 255 (Part Ibrahimpatnam Mandal, Adibatla, Telangana 501510. Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/ndt4YV8baNWaZu3M9

  • The event is kindly being hosted by Tata Consultancy Services – THANK YOU!
  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @Rekha_DR  and @shrinivasan_neelamegam 

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 25 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. About 2 weeks before the event, you will be asked to confirm your attendance and will receive further instructions.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud enables user-based authoriza...
entry.content:
Hello @Joey3, 

That’s unfortunate (and to be honest doesn’t seem logic to me) that Net Value cannot be hidden via access level configuration.

So, to summarise the solution to this issue:

User-based authorization control for sales staff can be partially achieved by using Authorization Group for Product as restrictions. However, the Net Value cannot be hidden via access level configuration as it is not part of the price elements area controlled by access levels (3744032 - Authorization restrictions in the field Net Value - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me)

👉  If you create an improvement request please link it here in a comment and ideally tag me – I would vote for it to support this topic 😊

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: Re: Workflow Trigger for Credit Memo (Document Type DG) i...
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (version 2502.602), it is not currently feasible to trigger a workflow specifically for Credit Memo postings (document type DG) created via the Create Outgoing Invoices app. Standard workflow capabilities in the Public Cloud are limited to predefined scenarios, and custom workflow triggers for this app are not supported. As of now, there is no published roadmap item confirming future support for this functionality, to my knowledge.
The only path forward is either external workflow automation or raising a roadmap influence request with SAP.

Good day @rohini_dutta21 

Thank you for your question.

From what you described, the most like reason for what you are experiencing is not the planning horizon itself, but the fact that the project reporting is still picking up project relevant actual line items that originated from your migration or follow on accounting logic such as credits or settlements. Note that project monitoring compares planned and actual values for project financial performance, and project costs are driven by relevant project costs that come from line items. See KBAs 3435982 and 3524410 for more details.

Below are answers to your questions.

Question 1: Why these negative values are still being reflected despite the year filter?

Answer: As mentioned above, the simplest explanation for that is that the reporting app is still reading project relevant actual posting that are still assigned to the project and are therefore included in the actual cost figures used by reporting. Since the year filter will not always exclude every earlier source posting in the way you want it in Monitor Projects, I would not position this as a filter defect without first checking the underlying line items. In practice, if migrated 2025 values are still part of the reporting basis for your project, they can continue to influence the actual cost figures you see in project monitoring until you offset the underlying accounting data correctly.

Question 2: Is this behavior expected due to migration, settlements, or currency handling?

Answer:

  • Migration: yes. You already found migration related G/L posting with negative value.
  • Settlement or credits: also plausible. Actual costs can be settled from a project or WBS to receivers. That means negative values are not automatically wrong in project reporting, they can also be because of settlement or correction.
  • Currency handling: this is possible but less likely to be the primary cause since you already identified negative migration postings in the ledger. The differences between project currency, company code currency, and global currency in project cost reports and KBA 3407757 recommends checking Actual Costs in Company Code Currency through line item drilldown when you experience inconsistent currency display.

Question 3: Could the extended planning and commitments up to 2027 be influencing this behavior?

Answer: They can influence the variance result, but they should not create actual cost by themselves. Extending planning and commitments into 2024 can affect the variance calculation, but not the existence of the negative actual cost itself. The negative actual cost still needs to come from accounting postings.

So, the distinction here is:

  • Negative actual cost is driven by actual project relevant postings.
  • Variance impact is influenced by the relationship between plan and actual, so your future plans and commitments can change the variance view.

Question 4: How can this issue be resolved or corrected?

Answer: I suggest you trace the exact project relevant line items (make use of the Project Cost Report - Plan/Actual app) and then correct the underlying source postings, rather than trying to fix this only from the Monitor Projects screen.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: HANA does not retain table partitioning when a column...
entry.content:
Hi 

Thanks for the update . 

Adding a column does NOT merge the Partitions at Hana Client. 

 

I have performed the below test : 

1) Created a test table -

create table I1 ( a int, b varchar(10), c varchar(10))

 

2) Partitioned the table :

Alter table I1

PARTITION BY HASH (a)

PARTITIONS 1,

RANGE (b)

(PARTITION VALUE = '2024000',

PARTITION VALUE = '2024001',

PARTITION VALUE = '2024002',

PARTITION VALUE = '2024003',

PARTITION VALUE = '2024004',

PARTITION OTHERS);

 

 

3) Inserted the test data :

insert into I1 values (100,'2024000', 'Good');

insert into I1 values (200,'2024000', 'Good');

insert into I1 values (300,'2024000', 'Good');

insert into I1 values (400,'2024000', 'Good');

insert into I1 values (1000,'2024001', 'Good');

 

4) Checked the Table and partitions :

SELECT * FROM I1;

select * from M_CS_PARTITIONS where TABLE_NAME='I1'

 

5) Added a new Column :

ALTER TABLE I1 ADD ("NEW_C" VARCHAR(10) );

UPDATE I1 SET NEW_C = 'NEW_VAL';

 

6)Checked the Table and partitions :

SELECT * FROM I1;

select * from M_CS_PARTITIONS where TABLE_NAME='I1'

 

The same table partitions appeared .

 

~Gaurav

entry.title: Re: Ecoembes Reusable Elements fee calcualtion
entry.content:
The fee in Packaging Fees app will always show the full fee, because Packaging Fees app shows information that is based on the packaging data only and not on the logistics data.

Analyze Report Data will show the raw inbound and outbound quantities, which are used for the statistical calculation.

Therefore in Analyze Report Data you will also see the full fees in each of the involved report logistic processes.

The only place where the calculated quantity of first-time use is visible is in Datos Elementos Envases - NEW_REUTI_QT.

Based on this NEW_REUTI_QT, Ecoembes will charge the company the respective amount.

There's no way in RDP to see the charged amount for reusable packaging.

The statistical calculation is based on the bought empty reusable packaging and delivered full reusable packaging.

If a company buys 100 pallets and then for the whole year makes deliveries involving 1000 pallets, the system will assume that 100 of the pallets were brand new and will put 100 in the NEW_REUTI_QT. All other 900 pallets that were used in deliveries will be assumed to be either these 100 (rotated 9 times each) or other already used pallets from previous periods.

There is a page in the help documentation for the Ecoembes report which describes the logic for calculating NEW_REUTI_QT - "Reporting Reusable Packaging Data".

entry.title: Re: SAP RDP Ecoembes fees for industrial packaging
entry.content:
Ecoembes, as PRO, does not manage industrial packaging directly, but has B2B agreements with other PROs, and offers one-stop shop solution for its members.
See here
https://ecoembesempresas.com/en/industrial-packaging
 
As result, RDP cannot know the fees for the industrial packaging, as this packaging will be reported to Envalora, Genci, Carton Circular, Implica or Sun Repack.
As far as we understand, the industrial packaging supplied to the market can be reported to Ecoembes, together with the household packaging, who in turn will forward the information about the industrial packaging to the other PRO. The other PRO will issue an invoice and Ecoembes will forward it to the customer.
Whether the company uses this flow (one-stop shop with Ecoembes) or deals directly with the other PRO is up to them.
If they need to report the data for industrial packaging to another PRO, this will need to be a user-defined report.
 
In general, a company in Spain can have contracts with multiple PROs for the different types of packaging they put on the market. Some companies may split the obligations to multiple PROs on the "usage" axis (household vs. comercial), while others may do it on the "hazardousness" axis (packaging of hazardous products in one PRO, normal packaging in another PRO)

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALMActivate work modes to prevent unavailab...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Thanks for reaching out to SAP Support.

You can manage business services, assigned cloud services and technical systems, and related events In the Business Service Management application in SAP Cloud ALM for operations.

Kindly check https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/business-service-management.html?anchorId=section for details:

The following features is provided: 

  • Manage status events
    • You can display and process the cloud service status events (Disruption, Degradation, Maintenance, Communication), which are notified in SAP for Me(opens in new tab) in the aspect of the corresponding business services. This allows you to see which business functionality is - or was - affected by a downtime event of a cloud service. 
    • For private cloud systems, Business Service Management displays additionally  Planned Downtime*, Planned Availability, Non Service Hours in the context of the associated business service. These event types are also displayed in the Private Cloud Workspace(opens in new tab) in SAP for Me.
    • You can define own status events on cloud services and technical systems. This is especially useful for your on-premise systems, which are not covered by SAP in the Cloud Availability Center, as they are operated by the customer. Here you can define e.g. planned maintenances or service disruptions for your own systems and communicate this to your end users.

Business Service Management forwards upcoming and ongoing cloud service or system status events (disruption, degradation, maintenance) to the SAP Cloud ALM monitoring applications. Health Monitoring will suppress event actions (create alert, send mail, create ticket, start operation flow) during maintenances and disruptions.

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Rules for replicating WBS elements to MDI - sap s/4hana cloud public edition
entry.content:
You want to replicate WBS elements from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition-S4HC to MDI client. However, you notice that while some WBS elements are replicated, some are not, some are with error message.

Here are the expected error message during replication:

A. Replication Aborted
1. 327(/s4ppm/crud) Element &1 not processed by MDI. Not an account assignment element

This message represents:
  1.a All milestones for Enterprise Project will not be replicated.
  1.b All project level for Professional Services Project will not be replicated.

2. 328(/s4ppm/crud)Element &1 not processed by MDI. All funcions are blocked
  2.a All workpackages for Professional Services, where the project is in the status of 'In Planning' will not be replicated. As its block functions are all set as 'Blocked'.
  2.b All WBS Element(Enterprise Project)/Workpackges(Professional Services) with processing status 'Locked(20)' and 'Closed(42)' will not be replicated. 

B. Replication posted with error messages, some fields are empty:
1. 325(/S4PPM/CRUD) System language &1 is a non-ISO language; element name not processed.
  The system language is not an ISO language, so element name will not be replicated
2. 077(WFD_WP1_MSG) Business partner does not exist for the user &1.
  The employee who is assigned as project manager has not been replicated to MDI workforce. So the project manager info will not be replicated.

entry.title: How user can export to XLSX from SALV full screen mode
entry.content:
By default, the smallest code implementing CL_SALV_TABLE in fullscreen mode (see code below) proposes a button to export the ALV to Excel but it's triggering the old Excel XXL List Viewer:

All other buttons don't propose the export I want:

Only the context menu from the grid view > Spreadsheet proposes something close to what I want, but still one button is missing and the other buttons:

What I want precisely in the full screen mode:

  1. Remove the button XXL List Viewer or make it start directly the "Export As xlsx-LEAN-STANDARD Microsoft Excel (*.xlsx)"
  2. Replace the export button by the one proposed by CL_SALV_TABLE in container mode (in the code below, see what it is by uncommenting the line « + 1 " Container mode »)
  3. The code must be as short as possible

My system is ABAP 7.58 SP 1 ON-PREMISE.

Thank you.

Sandra

NB: this question was already asked (ABAP) ALV grid "Spreadsheet" export button vs "Mi... - SAP Community but the answer is not exactly the one I expect and the thread is currently locked.

Code:

REPORT zzs.
DATA go_salv    TYPE REF TO cl_salv_table.
DATA gt_sflight TYPE TABLE OF sflight.

PARAMETERS dummy.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
  IF go_salv IS NOT BOUND.
    SELECT *
      FROM sflight
      INTO TABLE @gt_sflight.
    CASE 0 " Full screen mode
*         + 1 " Container mode
          .
      WHEN 0.
        " Full screen mode
        cl_salv_table=>factory( IMPORTING r_salv_table = go_salv
                                CHANGING  t_table      = gt_sflight ).
      WHEN 1.
        " Container mode
        cl_salv_table=>factory( EXPORTING r_container  = cl_gui_container=>screen0
                                IMPORTING r_salv_table = go_salv
                                CHANGING  t_table      = gt_sflight ).
    ENDCASE.
    go_salv->get_functions( )->set_all( ).
    go_salv->display( ).
  ENDIF.

entry.title: Re: How user can export to XLSX from SALV full screen mode
entry.content:
In the full screen mode, either you use the standard solution to use modern XLSX:

  • context menu > Spreadsheet (as shown in the question)
  • List menu > Export > Spreadsheet
  • type &XXL in the command field

Or use this custom solution:

  • Solution 1: use a copy of the standard toolbar (GUI Status) that you adapt to make the Export button run the function code &XXL instead of &VEXCEL. Precisely:
    • Duplicate the GUI Status SALV_TABLE_STANDARD of function group SALV_METADATA_STATUS into your custom program
    • In the application toolbar, replace the function codes &VGRID &VEXCEL &VLOTUS &VCRYSTAL_SL &GRAPH with &XXL only
    • In your program, add salv->set_screen_status( report = sy-repid pfstatus = 'SALV_TABLE_STANDARD' ).
  • Solution 2: keep the default standard toolbar and redirect programmatically &VEXCEL to &XXL via the event BEFORE_SALV_FUNCTION of CL_SALV_TABLE.
    • It requires to use SET_USER_COMMAND of CL_GUI_ALV_GRID and so to obtain its instance from CL_SALV_TABLE, that is impossible in standard.

Code for solution 2:

REPORT ztest.
CLASS lcl_app DEFINITION DEFERRED.
DATA app TYPE REF TO lcl_app.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  CREATE OBJECT app TYPE ('LCL_APP').
  CALL METHOD app->('MAIN').

CLASS lcl_app DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    CLASS-METHODS get_grid
      IMPORTING container     TYPE REF TO cl_gui_container
      RETURNING VALUE(result) TYPE REF TO cl_gui_alv_grid.
    METHODS main.
    METHODS on_before_salv_function
      FOR EVENT before_salv_function
                  OF cl_salv_events_table
      IMPORTING e_salv_function sender.
    DATA salv          TYPE REF TO cl_salv_table.
    DATA table_sflight TYPE TABLE OF sflight.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    DATA alv_grid TYPE REF TO cl_gui_alv_grid.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS lcl_app IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD get_grid.
    DATA controls TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF REF TO cl_Gui_Control.
    controls = value #( ( container ) ).
    LOOP AT controls INTO DATA(control).
      TRY.
          APPEND LINES OF CAST cl_gui_container( control )->children TO controls.
        CATCH cx_root ##NO_HANDLER.
          " control is not CL_GUI_CONTAINER
      ENDTRY.
      TRY.
          result = CAST cl_gui_alv_grid( control ).
          RETURN.
        CATCH cx_root ##NO_HANDLER.
          " control is not CL_GUI_ALV_GRID
      ENDTRY.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD main.
    SELECT * FROM sflight INTO TABLE @table_sflight.
    cl_salv_table=>factory( IMPORTING r_salv_table = salv
                            CHANGING  t_table      = table_sflight ).
    salv->get_functions( )->set_all( ).
    DATA lo_events TYPE REF TO cl_salv_events_table.
    lo_events = salv->get_event( ).
    SET HANDLER on_before_salv_function FOR lo_events.
    salv->display( ).
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD on_before_salv_function.
    IF e_salv_function = '&VEXCEL'.
      DATA(grid) = get_grid( cl_gui_container=>screen0 ).
      grid->set_user_command( '&XXL' ).
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

entry.title: Understanding Procure-to-Pay (P2P) Cycle in SAP FICO
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP FICO plays a key role in connecting procurement activities with financial accounting. One of the best examples of this integration is the Procure-to-Pay (P2P) cycle.

Process Flow

Purchase Requisition
↓
Purchase Order
↓
Goods Receipt
↓
Invoice Receipt
↓
Payment

Key Financial Impact

At Invoice Stage:

  • Debit: Expense / Inventory

  • Credit: Vendor (Accounts Payable)

At Payment Stage:

  • Debit: Vendor

  • Credit: Bank

Why It Matters

  • Ensures accurate financial recording

  • Tracks vendor liabilities

  • Improves cash flow control

  • Reduces manual errors

Conclusion

The P2P cycle shows how SAP FICO integrates operations with finance, making business processes more efficient and reliable. For beginners, understanding this flow is a strong foundation in SAP.

entry.title: Re: Missing Revenue Recognition Key - Professional Services
entry.content:
Hello @tomlovelock 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, there is a standard way to correct the scenario you described after the project has already been created, and it does not automatically require that you create a new WBS element. You can achieve that with Change Revenue Recognition Key capability in Event-Based Revenue Recognition-projects and also via integration API API_EVENT_BASED_REVREC_PROJECT_SRV through the ChangeRecognitionKey action. This allows you to change the revenue recognition key originally assigned to a WBS element. See SAP Note 3220122 

That means your intended preventive check with the validation BAdI is a good idea for avoiding future errors, but for your existing project the standard correction path is to assign or change the revenue recognition key on the affected billing WBS element rather than rebuilding the project structure from scratch.

The missing postings are because the revenue recognition key is not being derived from the WBS element. You must maintain the correct combination in the configuration of Derivation of Recognition Key for Projects and when you assign sales order items the same WBS element, they must derive the same revenue recognition key.

So, in your case, if you introduced new products and didn’t update the derivation logic for the new product and item category combination, the system can leave the billing WBS element without a revenue recognition key, which then prevents revenue recognition postings from being created.

In summary, the standard route I would recommend is:

  1. Correct the derivation config first by updating Derivation of Recognition Key for Projects so future products automatically derive the correct key.
  2. Correct the existing affected WBS by using Event-Based Revenue Recognition-Projects to assign or change the revenue recognition key on the billing WBS element.                                                      

     

  3. Use the Run Revenue Recognition-Projects app to reprocess revenue recognition after the key is corrected.

For further references,  consult:

3427750 - Project with Revenue without Recognition Key - SAP for Me

3475226 - Issues with the determination of the recognition key during sales order creation for a project assigned to two revenue recognition keys - SAP for Me

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/89d896ca9cd64318b1667df5ec00e4b2/1ab3aa92c5df43a9aca1cc2ed437a375.html?locale=en-US

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: SAP PP Rework Order: How to Post 101 GR for Reworked Quantity
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a question regarding rework processing in SAP PP.

I created a production order for quantity 10 with an operation using control key PP03, and the trigger point for “rework order generation” is activated.

During confirmation, I posted a yield of 8 and sent the remaining 2 to rework. A 101 goods receipt was posted for the 8 pieces, and a rework order was automatically generated for the remaining 2 pieces.

I have now completed confirmation for the rework order. However, as we know, we cannot directly post a 101 goods receipt against the rework order.

My question is: how is the goods receipt (101) for the reworked quantity (2 pieces) typically handled in this scenario? What is the standard or recommended process in SAP PP for receiving the reworked quantity into stock?

Thanks in advance for your guidance.

Rahul Singh

entry.title: [S/4 HANA Public Cloud] eDocuments created for Peppol customer invoiced with other company code
entry.content:

Because Peppol is currently not cross border, only BE to BE should be sent over the Peppol network or DE to DE, not DE to BE.

In the schedule edocument jobs we setup a job to submit the e-invoices automatically, this will then also submit the invoices from DE  to BE customer A which we don't want.

How can we prevent this? 

 

Thanks.

Best regards

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: [S/4 HANA Public Cloud] eDocuments created for Peppol...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

 

Peppol is explicitly allowing cross-border, via BIS Billing 3.0 profile. So this is a mandatory scenario.

Two options can be selected:

  1. Use EDOC_ADAPTOR_CLOUD BADI Implementation for XML dat... - SAP Community and IS_RELEVANT method.
  2. Wait for an update which will introduce "Assign Party ID Types to Business Partner" configuration to restrict Peppol assignments to specific company codes. This may be released with ~ 2608, if all tests pass accordingly.

 

Best regards,

SAP Technical Support, Public Cloud ERP

entry.title: Re: SAP CALM Health Monitoring
entry.content:
Hello Nidhi,

The following necessary entries need to be confirmed and maintained in white list for SAP managed systems:

  1. Build your Allow List URL data:
    1. Open your SAP Cloud ALM API service key (accessed via the BTP cockpit "SAP Cloud ALM" sub account, or the SAP Cloud ALM Landscape Management app).
    2. Find the "url" field/attribute (e.g. "https://<sub-domain>.authentication.<region>.hana.ondemand.com")
    3. Use the data from the "url" field/attribute to populate the placeholders in these URLs:
      • <SAP Cloud ALM sub-domain>.authentication.<region>.hana.ondemand.com
      • <region>.alm.cloud.sap

  2. Contact SAP Private Cloud and request that they update the allow list with the URLs you built above:
    • For authentication to <SAP Cloud ALM sub-domain>.authentication.<region>.hana.ondemand.com
    • For CALM to <region>.alm.cloud.sap

For systems hosted by SAP,  proxy service  3128 would be required. 

For details, please review the following online documents:
Configuration page SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Suite 7
>Network Prerequisies
3454314 - What to request of SAP Private Cloud (formerly ECS/HEC) when they need to allow SAP Cloud ALM Registration RFC to use Proxy on port 3128
3106170 - SAP Cloud ALM: Register error "NIECONN_REFUSED(-10)" while connecting ABAP systems (S/4HANA, Business Suite, SAP NetWeaver Application Server for ABAP systems)

Best regards,
Ellie

entry.title: Picking sequence: Pick, pack, pass
entry.content:
Hi SAP EWM Community,

we use Pick, Pack, Pass in the warehouse and essentially have three activity areas:

Activity Area A
Activity Area B
Activity Area C

They are joined under 1 Higher Level Activity Area ABC. 

For production supply, the optimal picking sequence is A → B → C as Production is next to C. For distribution, our optimal sequence would be C → B → A as Loading Area is next to A. 

We haven’t yet found a way to change the picking sequence based on the process, e.g., warehouse process type.

Is this possible within SAP Standard ? Does anyone have an idea?

Thank you in advance,
Nicolas

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps Deprecation and The Path Forward
entry.content:
Hi @patrick_schad,

the advice for backend applications

SAP Build Apps Backend Projects

To transfer existing data from SAP Build Apps backend projects into the unified SAP Build offering, we recommend a manual approach:

In your SAP Build Apps backend project, open each entity in the data browser and use the Export option to download your data as a .csv file. Repeat this step for each entity.

In your new CAP project within SAP Build, import the downloaded .csv files. (Prerequisite: ensure that a table with the relevant fields has already been created in your CAP backend.)

For detailed instructions on uploading data into your CAP backend, please refer to this guide.

has a major caveat. In CAP the CSV files contain "initial data" or test data depending on the location in the project where you place your CSV files. If a table has a CSV file with initial data, each deployment of the application restores the initial data from the CSV file. If users change the data via the app, the changes will be lost!

Without detailed knowledge of CAP, this approach can lead to unintended consequences.

Kind regards,
Sebastian

entry.title: Re: how to setup automatic invoices from edocument cockpit
entry.content:
KSeF integration is supported through SAP’s DRC solution. Instead of manually downloading XMLs, configure the eDocument Cockpit to automatically generate, send, and receive invoices via the KSeF API.

SAP provides pre-delivered content for Poland’s KSeF. You need to activate the relevant communication scenario in your SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system (e.g., SAP_COM_0527 or similar, depending on release). This sets up secure communication between your system and the KSeF portal.

Define the communication system (pointing to KSeF). Configure the communication arrangement with authentication (certificate/token as required by KSeF). This ensures invoices flow automatically without manual upload.
